message:
perf(storage): cache conversation tail state on the conversation row

Adds two new conversation columns:

- last_message_idx          INTEGER  (max(messages.idx) for the conversation)
- last_message_created_at   INTEGER  (max(messages.created_at) for the conversation)

Append-only merges previously had to issue a `SELECT MAX(idx),
MAX(created_at) FROM messages WHERE conversation_id = ?` for every
incoming conversation merge to figure out where to append. With many
conversations and many messages, that aggregate runs once per merge
even though the answer is fully determined by the last insert this
process did. The new columns let us read the tail state in O(1) off
the conversations row, and update it incrementally when we append.

Schema:

- CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION bumped 14 → 15.
- New `MIGRATION_V15` adds the two columns and back-fills them with
  the existing `MAX(messages.idx)` / `MAX(messages.created_at)` values
  per conversation. Registered in `build_cass_migrations()`.
- The combined fresh-schema CREATE TABLE for `conversations` includes
  the new columns directly so v15-or-later databases never have to run
  the migration.

Helpers and call sites:

- `conversation_tail_state(&Conversation)` /
  `borrowed_messages_tail_state(&[&Message])` compute (max_idx,
  max_created_at) in one pass over the message list.
- `franken_update_conversation_tail_state(...)` is the single point
  that updates ended_at / last_message_idx / last_message_created_at.
  It uses CASE-WHEN expressions so each candidate is independent: NULL
  candidates leave the existing value alone, non-NULL candidates take
  the per-column MAX. This preserves the existing ended_at behavior
  exactly while folding the two new columns into the same UPDATE.
- `franken_insert_conversation` populates last_message_idx /
  last_message_created_at on initial insert, computed from the
  conversation being inserted.
- `franken_existing_conversation_append_tail_state` reads the cache
  first and only falls back to the MAX() aggregate if the cache is
  NULL (covers conversations that exist from before v15 in databases
  that skipped the migration's UPDATE for any reason). The fallback
  back-fills the cache before returning, so the slow path is taken at
  most once per conversation.
- All four merge paths (append-only, profiled append-only, batched
  merge, and the duplicate-conversation recovery path inside the
  batched merge) now go through `franken_update_conversation_tail_state`
  instead of issuing their own `UPDATE conversations SET ended_at = ...`.
  The new helper is a strict superset of the old behavior — it still
  applies the MAX(IFNULL(ended_at, 0), ?) to ended_at — and now also
  threads the inserted-message tail state through.

Tests:

- `insert_conversation_tree_rehydrates_append_tail_state_cache_after_manual_clear`
  inserts a conversation, asserts the cache is populated, manually
  NULLs the two cache columns, runs an append, and asserts the cache
  is repopulated to the new tail. This exercises the
  read-fallback-then-rewrite branch in
  `franken_existing_conversation_append_tail_state`.
- `tests/storage.rs::conversations_table_has_correct_columns` is
  extended to require the two new columns.
